questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The principle of impulse-momentum relationship is used to predict the effect of a force on an object.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It states that the impulse applied to an object is equal to the change in its momentum.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the impulse-momentum relationship indeed predicts the effect of a force on an object, and the impulse applied is equal to the change in its momentum.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The centre of mass of an object is a point that represents the average position of its mass.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It remains at rest or moves with a constant velocity when no external forces act on the object.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the centre of mass indeed represents the average position of an object's mass, and it remains at rest or moves with a constant velocity when no external forces act on it.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The ground reaction force is an example of an external force that acts on the body.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
